zh.Farmborough, Warwickshire, England: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾
zh.Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾卡農斯 阿什比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Raleigh於1507年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾卡農斯 阿什比時，她的父親Sir Edward Raleigh34歲，母親Anne Chamberlain29歲。她於1537年與John Radford I在英國戴文洛可亞雷結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1558年1月16日在英國享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.Farmborough, Warwickshire, England: 英国英格兰瓦維其可爾
zh-hans.Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾卡農斯 阿什比
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Raleigh于1507年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾卡農斯 阿什比时，她的父亲Sir Edward Raleigh34岁，母亲Anne Chamberlain29岁。她于1537年与John Radford I在英国戴文洛可亞雷结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1558年1月16日在英国享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Raleigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Raleigh (House) in Pilton (Devon), recorded as Radeleia in 1086 and Rawlegh in 1535. The placename probably derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The English explorer Sir Walter Raleigh (1554–1618) was born in Hayes Barton, Devon, into a family of Devon gentry. He was related to most of the West Country's important families, including that of Sir Francis Drake. His half-brother was the explorer Sir Humphrey Gilbert. In 1578 Raleigh was granted a patent to explore and colonize “unknown lands” in America. In the 1580s he was granted extensive estates in Munster (Ireland), in reward for his suppression of the Desmond Rebellions.

variants.name: Rawleigh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Orlo.
English: variant of   Raleigh  , reflecting a common pronunciation.

variants.name: Riley
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name chiefly from High Ryley in Accrington (Lancashire) and Riley in Kirkburton, which was in the manor of Wakefield (Yorkshire). In some cases the name may derive from Riley Farm in Eyam (Derbyshire), Royley in Royton (Lancashire), or Rylah in Scarcliffe (Derbyshire). The placenames all come from Old English rȳge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish: from Ó Raghailligh ‘descendant of Raghailleach’, Old Irish Roghallach. This is the name of a chieftain family in Cavan, related to the O'Rourkes. In Anglicized form it has also been confused with Ó Raithile found in Munster, usually Anglicized as O'Rahilly or Rahilly . See O'Reilly .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Raleigh was born in 1507, in Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir Edward Raleigh, was 34 and her mother, Anne Chamberlain, was 29. She married John Radford I in 1537, in Rockbeare, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 16 January 1558, in England, at the age of 51, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Raleigh est née en 1507, à Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Raleigh, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Anne Chamberlain, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé John Radford I en 1537, à Rockbeare, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 janvier 1558, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Raleigh 1507 in Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Edward Raleigh 34 und ihre Mutter Anne Chamberlain 29. Sie hat 1537 in Rockbeare, Devon, England John Radford I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 16. Januar 1558 im Alter von 51 Jahren in England und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Raleigh è nata il nel 1507 nel Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Sir Edward Raleigh, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Anne Chamberlain, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato John Radford I nel 1537, nel Rockbeare, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 16 gennaio 1558, nel England, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Raleighが1507年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアカノンス アシュビーで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Raleighは34歳，母Anne Chamberlainは29歳であった。1537年にイギリスデボンロクベアアでJohn Radford Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1558年1月16日にイギリスで51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Raleigh(이)가 1507년 영국 노르섬프토실르 캔스 애쉬비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edward Raleigh(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Chamberlain(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1537년 영국 데폰 록브이르에서 John Radford I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1558년 1월 16일 영국에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Raleigh nasceu em 1507, em Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Raleigh, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Anne Chamberlain, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com John Radford I em 1537, em Rockbeare, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 16 de janeiro de 1558, em Inglaterra, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Raleigh родилась в 1507 г. в местности канонс ашби, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Edward Raleigh было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Chamberlain, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Radford I в 1537 г. в местности роккбир, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 16 января 1558 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Raleigh nació en 1507, en Canons Ashby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Raleigh, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Anne Chamberlain, tenía 29 años. Se casó con John Radford I en 1537, en Rockbeare, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 16 de enero de 1558, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
